evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Absolute stability is paired with minimal interfragmentary motion and primary bone healing.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Open-fracture antibiotic prophylaxis is a time-sensitive component of initial management.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Preserved pulses do not exclude acute compartment syndrome.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Document motor, sensory, pulse, and perfusion findings before and after reduction, splinting, or surgery.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Immediate life threats are addressed before definitive management of an extremity injury.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Evaluation of impaired union considers mechanical stability, biology, infection, host factors, and time course.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: A wound near a fracture should be evaluated for communication with the fracture rather than dismissed by wound size.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Post-reduction assessment includes alignment imaging and repeat skin and neurovascular examination.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Early postoperative trauma care includes neurovascular surveillance, wound and compartment assessment, mobilization restrictions, and prophylaxis planning.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Compartment pressure measurement is an adjunct when the examination is unreliable or equivocal and should not delay treatment of a clear clinical syndrome.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Urgent reduction of a gross deformity is followed by repeat neurovascular examination and documentation.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Relative stability permits controlled motion and commonly produces secondary bone healing with callus.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: A single normal neurovascular examination does not replace serial assessment when deterioration remains possible.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Definitive fixation timing is governed by both patient physiology and the condition of the soft-tissue envelope.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.claim_text: Initial splinting should maintain reduction while accommodating expected swelling and protecting skin.

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Clinical findings concerning for evolving acute compartment syndrome
metadata.action: Remove constriction, keep the limb appropriately positioned, perform serial examination, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Physiologic instability, severe soft-tissue injury, or unsafe definitive operative conditions
metadata.action: Choose temporary stabilization and defer definitive fixation until physiology and soft tissues permit

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Fracture with a communicating wound or high suspicion for communication
metadata.action: Cover the wound, document neurovascular status, initiate indicated antibiotics and tetanus assessment, and arrange operative debridement

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: New pain escalation, neurologic change, perfusion change, wound concern, or loss of alignment
metadata.action: Repeat focused examination and imaging as indicated and escalate promptly for a limb- or construct-threatening complication

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Unreliable or equivocal examination with ongoing concern for compartment syndrome
metadata.action: Use pressure measurement as an adjunct while maintaining serial assessment and urgent escalation

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Gross deformity, threatened skin, dislocation, or neurovascular compromise
metadata.action: Perform timely controlled reduction with appropriate analgesia or sedation and document post-reduction status

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Reduced or unstable acute injury requiring temporary support
metadata.action: Apply a well-padded splint that maintains alignment and accommodates swelling, then repeat examination

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Simple reconstructible pattern versus comminuted or biologically vulnerable pattern
metadata.action: Select absolute or relative stability principles appropriate to fracture morphology and biology

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Failure of expected clinical or radiographic progression toward union
metadata.action: Evaluate mechanical, biologic, infectious, and host contributors before selecting revision treatment

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Hemodynamic instability or unresolved life threat
metadata.action: Prioritize resuscitation and life-saving intervention before definitive fracture fixation

evidence_summary: Trauma Fundamentals manufacture scope; evidence packet required

metadata.trigger: Absent pulse, impaired perfusion, expanding hematoma, or evolving ischemia
metadata.action: Reduce gross deformity when appropriate and escalate immediately for vascular evaluation and limb-saving management
